data.description:
## Finding (S3 — verified)
`src/rebar/__init__.py` is a 1224-LOC god-facade (largest file), exempt from the size gate by filename pattern, with ~49 top-level defs mostly delegating to `_reads`/`_commands`/`_writes`.

Citations: `src/rebar/__init__.py` (1224 LOC; 14-param `list_tickets` at :872 — the fat signature is handled by the TicketQuery ticket, not here).

## Remediation
Relocate the wrapper BODIES into their existing owning submodules; keep `__init__.py` as import-and-re-export only, preserving every `rebar.<name>` path. Split only along clusters that already hang together; do NOT create <100-LOC fragments. After the split, pin `__init__.py` to a ceiling row (see S1 ratchet ticket).

cascade_flag: moves ~49 symbol DEFINITIONS (names re-exported) — check anything importing `rebar._<oldloc>` directly, star-imports, and `__all__`; verify `dir(rebar)` unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `__init__.py` is a re-export surface under the pinned ceiling; logic lives in topical submodules
- [ ] All public `import rebar; rebar.<fn>` names resolve unchanged (dir(rebar) stable)
- [ ] No file <100 LOC created by the split
data.title: Thin the __init__.py god-facade behind its existing submodules
